title: satureja microphylla subsp. filiformis
intro: Satureja microphylla subsp. filiformis, also known as Creeping Savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Satureja microphylla subsp. filiformis include its ability to aid digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and help alleviate cold and flu symptoms. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be used fresh in cooking and as a garnish.
